rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Niesar & Vestal Llp, Gerald Niesar, And Oscar Escobar'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission And Related Discovery (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/21/19)
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 3, 2) Defendants Niesar & Vestal Llp, Gerald Niesar, And Oscar Escobar'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission And Related Discovery (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/21/19) Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is inclined to deny the motion. Moving party's motion improperly combined three (3) separate motions to compel further responses. Moving party's Separate Statements fail to comply with CRC 3.1345 in that the separate statements are not "full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response" and did not include "the text of all definitions, instructions, and other matters required to understand each discovery request and the responses to it." CRC 3.1345(c). The court is also inclined to deny the request for sanctions because the notice of motion did not specify the person(s) against whom sanctions are sought as required by C.C.P. 2023.030. The court notes that trial in this matter is set for September 16, 2019, and moving party's last-minute motion and the lack of reasonable meet and confer in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions And Related Discovery
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 3, 3) DEFENDANTS GERALD NIESAR, OSCAR ESCOBAR, NIESAR & VESTAL, LLP'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions And Related Discovery Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is inclined to deny the motion without prejudice. Moving party's Separate Statements fail to comply with CRC 3.1345in that the separate statements are not "full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response" and did not include "the text of all definitions, instructions, and other matters required to understand each discovery request and the responses to it." CRC 3.1345(c). The court is also inclined to deny the request for sanctions because the notice of motion did not specify the person(s) against whom sanctions are sought as required by C.C.P. 2023.040. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Challenge Defendants Claim Of Privilege And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 3, 1) PLAINTIFFS BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, IGNACIO GUTIERREZ's Motion To Challenge Defendants Claim Of Privilege And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Defendants should be prepared to address the issue of when a code-compliant claim of privilege / claw back was made pursuant to C.C.P. 2031.285. Plaintiffs should be prepared to address the issue of whether subject materials are on their face privileged materials. The parties should be prepared to address the issue of the identity of the holder of privilege ("PLM vis-à-vis Regent Pacific") and the relationship between Sonia Garcia and Regent Pacific. The court is inclined to deny the request for sanctions because the notice of motion did not specify the person(s) against whom sanctions are sought as required by C.C.P. 2023.030. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: JUNE E CLAUSEN VS. PACIFIC CRUISE SHIP TERMINALS LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel All Defendants, Responses To Plaintiff June E. Clausens, Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Two (2) And Request For Order Awarding Momentary Sanctions Against All Defendants In The Amount Of 2,750.00
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 5, 4) Motion To Compel All Defendants, Responses To Plaintiff June E. Clausens, Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Two (2) And Request For Order Awarding Momentary Sanctions Against All Defendants In The Amount Of 2,750.00 Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion is GRANTED, and defendants are to provide code-compliant responses without any objection within five (5) court days of notice of entry of order. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,750.00 against all defendants, and payment of sanctions shall be made within five (5) court days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: EVELYN DAVIDSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Unredacted Records Received By Plaintiff Pursuant To Subpoena, Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Subpoena Service To Deliver Unredacted Documents Received Under Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Unredacted Records Received By Plaintiff Pursuant To Subpoena, Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Subpoena Service To Deliver Unredacted Documents Received Under Subpoena Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The court is inclined to grant the motion. Plaintiff's medical records as to the causes of the "garden variety" emotional distress claimed by Plaintiff are discoverable because Plaintiff has placed the symptoms at issue and thus entitling the Defendant to obtain evidence as to the cause of these symptoms. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating, verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E JONES III VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Deposition And Produce Documents In Deposition Notice (Continued From 8/20/19 Per Ex Parte Order File 8/9/19)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 3, 2019, Line 11, Motion To Compel Plaintiff Deposition And Produce Documents In Deposition Notice (Continued From 8/20/19 Per Ex Parte Order File 8/9/19). The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted, modified as follows. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel plaintiff's deposition is granted. Plaintiff shall make himself available for deposition no later than September 13, 2019. In addition, Plaintiff shall produce at least one week prior to the deposition the documents sought in the deposition notice served by the City on March 5, 2019, if they have not already been produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PRAYOOT JANTARADEJDUANGDEE VS. BILLION MICRO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: And Mortion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Monkol Jaemjaeng
rulings.Rulings: September 3, 2019 Line 4, PLAINTIFF PRAYOOT JANTARADEJDUANGDEE'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Monkol Jaemjaeng Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: KENT MAGNELL VS. ARROYO REALTY AND EDEVELOPMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 3, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT ARROYO REALTY AND DEVELOPMENT, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Arroyo Realty and Development, LLC's demurrer to plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained in part. The demurrer to the entire complaint on the basis of Bus. & Prof. Code, § 7031(a) is overruled; the demurrer to the third cause of action for violation of Pen. Code § 496(c) and the fourth cause of action for violation of the UCL is overruled; and the demurrer to the fifth cause of action for violation of the Federal Visual Artist Rights Act and the sixth cause of action for an accounting is sustained without leave to amend. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
